title: Rohan's Backwards Day
synopsis: Rohan decides to do everything backwards for a day, leading to hilarious mishaps and discoveries with his friends at school. When his backwards adventure causes problems, his diverse group of friends helps him find a creative solution that turns the day into the best backwards fun ever.

pages.text: Rohan woke up with a silly idea. "Today I will do everything backwards!" he said. He put on his shirt backwards. He put his shoes on the wrong feet. His mom laughed when she saw him. "Why are you dressed so funny?" she asked. "It's my backwards day!" Rohan said with a big smile.

illustration.description: A cheerful South Asian boy with messy black hair and big brown eyes putting on a striped shirt backwards in a colorful bedroom. His mother, a woman with kind eyes and long dark hair, is laughing in the doorway. Bright morning sunlight streams through the window. The room has toys scattered around and vibrant blue and yellow walls.
illustration.alt: Rohan putting his shirt on backwards while his mother watches and laughs

pages.text: At breakfast, Rohan tried to eat backwards too. He started with dessert cookies. Then he ate his cereal. "This is very strange," said his little sister Maya. She had pigtails and was only three years old. "But it looks fun!" Maya tried to eat her toast upside down. Crumbs fell everywhere. Their mom shook her head but kept smiling.

illustration.description: A cozy kitchen scene with Rohan eating cookies first while his little sister Maya holds her toast upside down, crumbs falling. Their mother stands nearby with an amused expression. The kitchen has warm orange and red tones with cheerful morning light. Maya has cute pigtails and big curious eyes just like her brother.
illustration.alt: Rohan and Maya eating breakfast in silly backwards ways

pages.text: "Don't be late for school," Mom said. Rohan had an idea. He walked backwards to the bus stop. Step by step, he moved backwards down the sidewalk. It was harder than he thought! He bumped into Mrs. Chen's mailbox. "Sorry!" he called, still walking backwards. Mrs. Chen waved and laughed. She was watering her flowers and thought Rohan was being very silly.

illustration.description: Rohan walking backwards down a suburban street, having just bumped into a colorful mailbox. An elderly Asian woman with gray hair and a sun hat is watering bright flowers in her garden, waving and smiling. The scene shows a sunny neighborhood with houses in soft pastels and a bright blue sky with fluffy clouds.
illustration.alt: Rohan walking backwards past Mrs. Chen who is watering her garden

pages.text: At school, Rohan's friends were very confused. "Why are you walking backwards?" asked his best friend Zara. She had beautiful curly red hair and freckles. "And why is your shirt backwards?" asked Marcus, who was tall and had dark skin and a bright smile. "It's my backwards day!" Rohan explained. "I'm doing everything backwards!" His friends thought this sounded like fun.

illustration.description: Three children standing in a bright school hallway with colorful lockers. Rohan in his backwards clothes faces his friends Zara, a girl with vibrant curly red hair and freckles, and Marcus, a tall Black boy with a wide friendly smile. Their expressions show curiosity and amusement. The hallway has cheerful educational posters and warm lighting.
illustration.alt: Rohan explaining his backwards day to his friends Zara and Marcus

pages.text: In class, Rohan tried to write backwards too. But it was very hard! His letters looked like squiggly worms. "This is impossible," he whispered to Zara. Their teacher, Ms. Rodriguez, noticed Rohan's strange writing. She had kind brown eyes and wore colorful jewelry. "Rohan, what's happening with your work today?" she asked gently. Rohan explained about his backwards day. The whole class started giggling.

illustration.description: A bright classroom with Rohan sitting at his desk looking frustrated at his backwards writing, which looks like squiggles on the paper. His teacher Ms. Rodriguez, a Latina woman with warm brown eyes and colorful earrings, leans over to help him. Other diverse children in the background are giggling. The classroom has educational posters and plants on the windowsill.
illustration.alt: Rohan struggling with backwards writing while Ms. Rodriguez helps him

pages.text: At lunch, things got even sillier. Rohan tried to eat his sandwich from the last bite to the first. He tried to drink his juice box upside down. Juice spilled all over his backwards shirt! "Oh no!" said Amara, who sat across from him. She had beautiful dark skin and wore bright yellow hair clips. "You're all sticky now!" All his friends started laughing, and Rohan laughed too, even though he was a mess.

illustration.description: A bustling school cafeteria with Rohan covered in juice, holding his sandwich upside down with a sheepish grin. His friend Amara, a Black girl with bright yellow hair clips, points at the mess with a concerned but amused expression. Other children at the lunch table are laughing. The cafeteria has warm lighting and colorful lunch trays.
illustration.alt: Rohan spilling juice on himself while trying to eat lunch backwards

pages.text: After lunch came art class. Rohan loved art! But doing art backwards was tricky. He tried to paint from right to left instead of left to right. His paintbrush went the wrong way. Paint splattered everywhere! "Whoops!" Rohan said as green paint landed on Emma's paper. Emma had blonde hair and usually didn't mind messes, but her beautiful flower painting was ruined. She looked sad.

illustration.description: An art classroom with easels and colorful supplies everywhere. Rohan holds a dripping paintbrush with paint splatters on his backwards shirt, looking apologetic. Emma, a girl with blonde hair in a ponytail, looks sadly at her flower painting now covered in green paint splatters. The room is bright with natural light and rainbow-colored art supplies.
illustration.alt: Rohan accidentally splattering paint on Emma's artwork in art class

pages.text: "I'm sorry, Emma," Rohan said quietly. He felt bad about ruining her painting. During recess, Rohan tried to play backwards tag. But he kept bumping into people! He accidentally knocked over his friend Jake, who had spiky brown hair and was running fast. Jake scraped his knee on the playground. "Ow!" Jake cried. Now Rohan felt even worse. Maybe backwards day wasn't such a good idea after all.

illustration.description: A playground scene with Rohan looking worried and guilty as Jake sits on the ground holding his scraped knee. Jake has spiky brown hair and looks hurt but not angry. Other children gather around looking concerned. The playground has bright equipment and green grass under a sunny sky, but the mood is more serious now.
illustration.alt: Rohan feeling guilty after Jake gets hurt during backwards tag

pages.text: Rohan sat on a bench feeling sad. His backwards day was causing problems for everyone. Zara sat down next to him. "What's wrong?" she asked kindly. "My backwards day is ruining everything," Rohan said with a sigh. "I made Emma's painting messy. I hurt Jake. I spilled juice everywhere. Maybe I should just go back to normal." He really wanted his backwards day to work, but it seemed impossible.

illustration.description: Rohan sitting sadly on a colorful playground bench with his head in his hands, still wearing his backwards clothes. Zara sits beside him with a concerned, caring expression, her curly red hair catching the sunlight. The playground scene behind them shows other children playing, with bright blues and greens creating a contrast to Rohan's subdued mood.
illustration.alt: Rohan sitting sadly on a bench while Zara comforts him

pages.text: "Wait!" said Zara with bright eyes. "I have an idea!" She called to Marcus, Emma, Amara, and Jake. Even with his hurt knee, Jake hopped over. "What if we ALL do things backwards?" Zara suggested. "Then Rohan won't be alone, and we can help each other!" Emma clapped her hands. "That sounds fun!" she said. "I forgive you about the painting, Rohan. Accidents happen." Jake nodded too. "Let's try it together!"

illustration.description: A circle of diverse children on the playground, all looking excited about Zara's idea. Zara stands in the center with her arms spread wide and a big smile. Emma claps her hands happily, Marcus grins widely, Amara bounces with excitement, and Jake sits on the ground but smiles despite his bandaged knee. The scene is full of energy and friendship with warm, bright colors.
illustration.alt: Zara sharing her idea with the group of friends who look excited

pages.text: All of Rohan's friends started doing things backwards too! Marcus walked backwards around the playground. Emma painted a new picture from right to left, making swirly designs. Amara tried to say her name backwards: "Arama!" Everyone giggled at how funny it sounded. Jake, even with his hurt knee, tried to hop backwards very carefully. "This is much more fun with friends!" Rohan said happily.

illustration.description: The playground filled with children doing silly backwards activities together. Marcus walks backwards with his arms out for balance, Emma paints swirly patterns, Amara has her hands cupped around her mouth saying her backwards name, and Jake hops carefully on one foot. Everyone is laughing and having fun. The scene is vibrant with motion and joy.
illustration.alt: All the friends doing backwards activities together on the playground

pages.text: Ms. Rodriguez came outside and saw all the children doing things backwards. She started laughing. "What a creative idea!" she said. "You're all helping each other learn something new!" She tried walking backwards too, but she was very wobbly. All the children cheered for their teacher. "Sometimes the best adventures happen when friends work together," she said with a warm smile. Rohan felt so much better now.

illustration.description: Ms. Rodriguez attempting to walk backwards on the playground while wobbling and laughing, her colorful jewelry jingling. All the diverse children cheer and clap around her in a semi-circle, their faces bright with joy and laughter. The teacher's expression is playful and kind. The scene captures the warmth of a teacher joining in her students' fun with rich, happy colors.
illustration.alt: Ms. Rodriguez trying to walk backwards while her students cheer

pages.text: For the rest of the day, everyone helped with backwards activities. In math class, they tried counting backwards from ten. "Ten, nine, eight..." they all said together. It was like a fun game! During story time, Ms. Rodriguez read the end of the book first, then the middle, then the beginning. "This is a very silly story!" said Marcus. Everyone agreed it was the most fun they'd ever had in school.

illustration.description: A cozy classroom with all the children sitting in a circle on a colorful rug, counting backwards with their fingers. Ms. Rodriguez holds a picture book and smiles at her engaged students. The room is warm and inviting with plants, books, and educational posters. Sunlight streams through windows creating a golden, happy atmosphere as everyone participates together.
illustration.alt: The class sitting in a circle practicing counting backwards together

pages.text: When the school day ended, Rohan felt amazing. "Thank you for helping me," he told all his friends. "My backwards day was only fun because you joined me." Zara grinned. "That's what friends do!" said Emma. "We help each other with crazy ideas!" added Marcus. Amara giggled. "Can we have a sideways day next?" Everyone laughed at that silly idea. Jake hopped up. "I'm ready for any adventure with you guys!"

illustration.description: The group of friends standing together outside the school building at the end of the day, all smiling and laughing. They have their arms around each other's shoulders in a friendly huddle. The school behind them is cheerful with bright windows and colorful decorations. The late afternoon sun creates a warm, golden glow around the friends, emphasizing their strong bond.
illustration.alt: The group of friends hugging and laughing together outside school

pages.text: Rohan walked home the normal way this time, but he was still wearing his backwards shirt. Maya was waiting for him. "How was your backwards day?" she asked excitedly. "It was the best day ever!" Rohan said. "But only because my friends helped me." That night at dinner, Rohan told his family all about his backwards adventure. "Tomorrow," he announced, "I think I'll try a normal day. But I'll always remember that friends make everything better!"

illustration.description: A warm family dinner scene with Rohan, still in his backwards shirt, animatedly telling his story to his parents and little sister Maya around a dinner table. His family listens with delighted expressions, leaning in with interest. The dining room glows with cozy evening light from a hanging lamp, and the table is set with a colorful meal. Everyone looks happy and content.
illustration.alt: Rohan telling his family about his backwards day adventure at dinner

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Rohan decide to do differently on his special day?
questions.explanation: Rohan decided to do everything backwards for the whole day, from putting on his shirt to walking to school!

questions.question: What problem did Rohan cause in art class?
choices: He splattered paint on Emma's picture
He ate all the art supplies
questions.explanation: When Rohan tried to paint backwards, his paintbrush went the wrong way and green paint splattered on Emma's beautiful flower painting.

questions.question: Who had the idea for everyone to do things backwards together?
questions.explanation: Zara came up with the great idea that all of Rohan's friends should do things backwards together so he wouldn't be alone and they could help each other.

questions.question: What did the friends try to count backwards from?
questions.explanation: In math class, all the friends practiced counting backwards from ten together: 'Ten, nine, eight...' like a fun game!

questions.question: What did Rohan learn about having adventures?
choices: They should always be done alone
questions.explanation: Rohan learned that adventures and trying new things are much more fun and successful when friends help and join in together.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instruction: Choose the right word to complete each sentence from Rohan's backwards day!

sentences.text: His friend _____ had curly red hair.
At lunch, Rohan spilled _____ on himself.

pages.title: Backwards Words
pages.instruction: Can you figure out these backwards words from the story?

sentences.text: nahoR decided to have a special day.
He walked sdrawkcab to the bus stop.
His sdneirf helped make the day better.
